transcript: Speaker A: Do you speak English? Speaker B: Yes. Speaker A: Prove it. Speaker B: Um, I'm here to visit my sister in Miami. I'm staying for two weeks. I have the return ticket. I'm not working, just visiting. Speaker A: All right. How? Speaker B: I used Loora. 10 minutes a day for real conversations like this one. Let me show you how it works. Speaker C: Hello, where are you flying? Speaker B: I fly London. Speaker C: 「I am flying to London」って言いたかったですか？ [Did you mean to say 'I am flying to London'?] Speaker B: Yes, sorry. I am flying to London. Speaker C: Any luggage to check in? Speaker B: Um, luggage? Speaker C: 「luggage」は、旅行に持っていくスーツケースやバッグのことです。 ['luggage' refers to suitcases or bags you take on a trip.] Speaker B: Oh, I have one luggage. ごめんなさい... 英語あんまり得意じゃなくて。 [Oh, I have one luggage. Sorry... I'm not very good at English.] Speaker C: 大丈夫ですよ！この1ヶ月、どのくらい英語を話しましたか？ [That's okay! How much English have you spoken this past month?] Speaker B: 正直... 全然話してなくて。 [Honestly... not at all.] Speaker C: じゃあ、どうやって英語ペラペラになろうと思ってるんですか？ [Then, how do you expect to become fluent in English?] Speaker B: 練習する相手がいなくて。 [I don't have anyone to practice with.] Speaker C: だったらLooraをダウンロードして、毎日私と話しましょう。思ってるより早く英語話せるようになりますよ。 [Then download Loora and talk to me every day. You'll be able to speak English faster than you think.] Speaker B: え、いいかも。今日から毎日使お。 [Oh, that sounds good. I'll use it every day starting today.]
on_screen_text: 英語は話せますか？ [Do you speak English?] はい。 [Yes.] それを証明してください。 [Prove it.] ええと、私はマイアミにいる姉を訪ねています。 [Um, I'm visiting my sister in Miami.] 2週間滞在する予定です。 [I plan to stay for two weeks.] 帰りの航空券も持っています。 [I also have a return ticket.] 働く予定はなく、ただ訪問しているだけです。 [I don't plan to work, just visiting.] なるほど。どうやって？ [I see. How?] Looraで、 [With Loora,] 1日10分だけ、リアルな会話の練習をしていました。 [I practiced real conversations for just 10 minutes a day.] どういう仕組みか、ちょっとお見せしますね。 [Let me show you how it works.] Hello, where are you flying? ❌ I fly London. 「I am flying to London」って言いたかったですか？ [Did you mean to say 'I am flying to London'?] Yes, sorry. I am flying to London. ✅ I am flying to London. Any luggage to check in? Luggage? 「luggage」は、旅行に持っていくスーツケースやバッグのことです。 ['luggage' refers to suitcases or bags you take on a trip.] Oh, I have one luggage. ごめんなさい... 英語あんまり得意じゃなくて。 [Sorry... I'm not very good at English.] 大丈夫ですよ！ [That's okay!] この1か月、どのくらい英語を話しましたか？ [How much English have you spoken this past month?] 正直... 全然話してなくて。 [Honestly... not at all.] じゃあ、どうやって英語ペラペラになろうと思ってるんですか？ [Then, how do you expect to become fluent in English?] 練習する相手がいなくて。 [I don't have anyone to practice with.] だったらLooraをダウンロードして、毎日私と話しましょう。 [Then download Loora and talk to me every day.] 思ってるより早く英語話せるようになりますよ。 [You'll be able to speak English faster than you think.] え、いいかも。今日から毎日使お。 [Oh, that sounds good. I'll use it every day starting today.] Loora
summary: A Japanese traveler passes passport control by speaking English learned via the Loora app. She demonstrates how the AI avatar corrects her grammar and explains vocabulary in Japanese. The ad encourages users to download Loora for daily English practice.
